statement: If we create a structured onboarding guide with links to all key docs in one place, then new hire time-to-first-PR will drop from 3-4 weeks to under 2 weeks, because most of the delay is spent searching for information rather than learning.
test_criteria: Survey new hires on onboarding experience

statement: If we implement a departure knowledge capture checklist, then critical context loss during offboarding will decrease, because structured prompts surface knowledge that would otherwise be forgotten.
assumptions: Departing employees are willing to participate
expected_outcome: Successor teams report having adequate context within first month
test_criteria: Reduced 'who knows about X?' questions post-departure
risks: Short-notice departures may not allow time

statement: If we create a weekly customer signal digest that routes feedback summaries to engineering leads, then engineering will incorporate customer pain points into sprint planning within 2 weeks of report, because the gap is awareness not willingness.
expected_outcome: Customer-reported issues appear in sprint backlogs within 2 weeks
test_criteria: Track time from customer report to backlog item
Engineering leads report being aware of top customer issues
risks: May surface more issues than capacity allows

statement: If we require a decisions log for every recurring meeting, then decision follow-through will improve, because people will have a single source of truth for what was decided and who owns next steps.
assumptions: Someone is willing to take notes
expected_outcome: Meeting action items have >80% completion rate within one week
risks: Note-taking burden falls on one person

statement: If we write runbooks for the top 5 incident types, then mean time to resolution will decrease by 40%, because responders currently spend most time figuring out what to do rather than doing it.
assumptions: Top 5 incident types cover 80% of incidents
Current responders can document their process
expected_outcome: MTTR drops from current average to 40% less
test_criteria: Track MTTR before and after runbook adoption
risks: Runbooks become outdated if not maintained

statement: If we implement a support ticket triage rotation where one engineer per week handles Tier 2 escalations, then average response time will drop from 5 days to under 1 day, because the bottleneck is ownership not capacity.
assumptions: One engineer's partial attention is sufficient
risks: Rotation engineer productivity on other work decreases

statement: If we track actual vs estimated story points for 3 sprints and review the data in retro, then estimation accuracy will improve by 25%, because teams will calibrate against real data instead of gut feel.
assumptions: Teams are willing to track actuals
expected_outcome: Sprint goal completion rate improves from 60-70% to 85%+
test_criteria: Sprint goal hit rate over 3 months
risks: May create pressure to game estimates
